title: Data Scientist - Commodities Trading - Hong Kong
matched_skills: Python, SQL, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, PySpark, Data Pipeline Engineering, Machine Learning, Fraud Detection, Risk Models

description:
Our client, a global asset management firm, are seeking a versatile, hands\-on Data Scientist to join the Commodities Trading desk. You will work closely with traders, quantitative researchers, portfolio managers and engineers to design and deploy data\-driven strategies, models and tools that improve trade selection, risk management, alpha generation and operational efficiency across physical and financial commodities (e.g., energy, metals, agriculture). This is a high\-impact role requiring strong domain curiosity, solid quantitative skills, production\-grade engineering, and the ability to communicate insights to non\-technical stakeholders.
 



  





**The role:** 



* Partner with traders and portfolio managers to identify trading opportunities and decision\-making bottlenecks that can be addressed via data science and analytics.
* Develop, validate and deploy predictive models (statistical, ML, time\-series) for forecasting prices, volatility, basis, seasonality, flows, inventories and other commodity\-specific signals.
* Build regime\-detection and market\-state models to adapt strategies across different volatility/liquidity environments.
* Implement risk models and stress\-test frameworks that quantify exposures (market, basis, carry, curve, roll) and assist real\-time position limits and scenario analysis.
* Engineer real\-time and batch data pipelines to ingest, clean, enrich and feature\-engineer heterogeneous data sources (market data, fundamentals, trade blotters, order book, news, satellite/alternative data).
* Work with software engineers to productionize models and integrate them into trading systems, dashboards and order management workflows (backtesting, live signals, alerts).
* Perform rigorous model validation, backtests, performance attribution, and documentation for internal review and regulatory requirements.
* Monitor model performance, recalibrate as needed, and proactively detect model drift or data quality issues.
* Conduct bespoke research projects (alpha discovery, cost\-of\-trade / transaction cost analysis, optimal execution, statistical arbitrage across commodities) and present findings to stakeholders.



  





**What you offer:** 



* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Quantitative discipline (Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Financial Engineering, Physics, or equivalent). PhD preferred but not required.
* 4\+ years of applied data science / quantitative modeling experience, ideally in commodities trading, energy markets, derivatives, or a related finance environment.
* Hands\-on programming experience in Python (pandas, numpy, scikit\-learn, statsmodels, PyTorch/TensorFlow desirable) and SQL for data manipulation and modeling.
* Experience with cloud platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure) and containerization (Docker) is a plus.
* Solid understanding of commodities market structure
* Excellent communication skills — able to present quantitative concepts and actionable recommendations to traders and senior management.



  





**The sell:** 



* Collaborative, fast\-paced environment with direct access to trading decision\-makers
* Competitive compensation package with performance\-linked incentives
* Work for a brand name that will help open doors for your career in the near future

description:

Our purpose and fight are what motivates us to design and deliver distinctive communications throughout our consumer funnel, so that we deliver category growth as well as a positive impact on society. With extensive media and digital teams embedded throughout the organisation, we measure both our business and our societal impact. Our global teams lead on our overarching brand strategy, brand guardianship and innovation pipeline in close partnership with R and D. With the scale of our 2500 marketing community \- alongside our leading\-edge Marketing Academy \- your opportunities to grow and upskill will be extensive.

We're on the lookout for a Global Insights \& Analytics Junior Analyst, whose creativity and analytical skills will help shape the future of our brands. description:
**Company Description** 




 We are a hard\-tech company focused on Micro LED transparent displays, spatial computing, and human–machine interaction interfaces. We are currently at a critical stage, transitioning from engineering validation to industrialization while preparing to initiate our Pre\-A to Series A financing. To accelerate both capital strategy clarity and product commercialization, we plan to engage high\-caliber external experts with a focus on two core modules.
 


1. The first module targets top\-tier investment professionals from leading global and regional funds with proven experience in semiconductors, display technologies, or AI hardware. Priority backgrounds include firms such as Sequoia Capital, IDG Capital, Matrix Partners China, Hillhouse Capital, Temasek Holdings, SoftBank Vision Fund, China Venture Capital Fund Corporation, and Qianhai Fund of Funds. These experts will support us in defining the optimal financing window, establishing a robust valuation framework, structuring capital efficiently, and mapping out realistic exit pathways such as IPO or M\&A.
2. 
3. The second module focuses on product application experts with hands\-on experience in product definition or technology adoption decisions. Target domains include smart wearables (e.g., Apple, Huawei, Xiaomi ecosystems), human–machine interaction in smart home (AIoT platforms), AI\-powered edge devices, automotive displays and intelligent cockpit systems (e.g., BYD, Huawei, Bosch ecosystems), as well as emerging display applications in AR glasses and smart helmets. The primary objective is to validate real market demand for transparent displays, identify the first viable commercialization scenarios, understand customer decision\-making processes, and define a scalable first product.



**Role Description** 




 All experts must have direct industry experience within the past three years, with strong preference for individuals who have been directly involved in technology selection, product commercialization, or investment decision\-making. The purpose of this expert engagement is not general information gathering, but to drive three critical outcomes: (1\) establish a compelling financing narrative and valuation anchor, avoiding the common low\-valuation trap of manufacturing\-driven businesses; (2\) identify a clear, executable product\-market fit and initial customer pathway; and (3\) proactively identify key risks across technology, cost, and commercialization. Ultimately, this process is designed to significantly enhance our strategic precision, execution efficiency, and probability of successful fundraising.
 



  





**Qualifications** 



* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills; ability to translate complex data into actionable strategies.
* Proficiency in business strategy development, market research, and competitor analysis.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with experience engaging stakeholders from diverse backgrounds.
* Knowledge of transparent Micro\-LED display technology, AR applications, or spatial computing is a significant advantage.
* Proven experience in consulting, business development, or a related role within the technology or display industries.
* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Technology, Engineering, or a related field; advanced degree is a plus.
* Ability to work independently in a remote environment while meeting deadlines and delivering high\-quality results.

description:
**About Basel Capital** 




 Basel Capital is a Hong Kong based investment and asset management platform focused on global real estate opportunities. The firm manages capital for ultra\-high\-net\-worth investors and family offices, with a strong track record in U.S. residential development.
 



  





  





**Position Overview** 




 We are seeking a highly driven Investment Analyst to support financial modeling, investor materials, and fundraising execution for U.S. real estate development projects.
 



 This role will play a key role in the final\-stage capital raise of an active development and will work directly with senior leadership on live transactions. The position offers significant exposure to cross\-border real estate investment and capital markets.
 



  





  





**Key Responsibilities** 




**1\. Financial Modeling \& Investment Analysis** 



* Build and refine detailed financial models for U.S. residential development projects
* Analyze returns (IRR, MOIC), perform sensitivity analysis, and structure capital stacks
* Support structuring of preferred equity, JV equity, and investor return profiles
* Work closely with U.S. team to validate assumptions based on real\-time project data



  





  





**2\. Fundraising Execution \& Investor Materials** 



* Lead preparation of investor\-facing materials, including:
* Offering Memorandums (OM)
* Pitch decks
* Investment summaries and teasers
* Translate financial analysis into clear, compelling investment narratives
* Support positioning of deals for high\-net\-worth and family office investors
* Participate in fundraising process, including responding to investor questions and data requests



  





  





**3\. Market Research \& Deal Support** 



* Conduct market research on U.S. residential real estate and comparable developments
* Support underwriting of new development opportunities and land acquisitions
* Assist in preparing internal investment memos and screening potential deals



  





  





**4\. Cross\-Border Coordination** 



* Coordinate closely with U.S.\-based development and execution teams
* Ensure alignment between financial models and actual project performance
* Support ongoing investor reporting and communications



  





  





**Qualifications (Required)** 



* 3–5 years of experience in:
* Investment banking, OR
* Real estate private equity / asset management, OR
* Real estate advisory or development firm
* **Direct experience in U.S. real estate fund / project is required**
* (e.g., involvement in capital raising, investor materials, or deal marketing)
* Strong financial modeling skills (Excel required)
* Solid understanding of investment concepts (IRR, MOIC, capital stack, waterfalls)
* Strong analytical and communication skills
* Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced, deal\-driven environment



  





  





**Preferred** 



* Experience with U.S. residential development (townhomes, condos, multifamily)
* Experience working with high\-net\-worth or cross\-border investors
* Bilingual in English and Chinese (Mandarin or Cantonese) preferred



  





  





**What We Offer** 



* Competitive compensation package with performance\-based bonus
* Direct involvement in a live U.S. development fundraising transaction
* Opportunity to work on cross\-border real estate investments
* Fast\-track career progression with potential path to senior investment role
* High level of responsibility and direct access to senior management



  





**How to Apply** 




 Please send your resume and (if available) a sample deal experience or financial model to:
 
 canny.zhang@baselcg.com

**About Preqin**
 This role sits within Preqin, a part of BlackRock. Preqin plays a key role in how we are revolutionizing private markets data and technology for clients globally, complementing our existing Aladdin technology platform to deliver investment solutions for the whole portfolio.
   

  

 You will be joining the world’s leading data and insights provider for the alternatives assets industry. We empower the finance community with comprehensive data and expert insights so they can make faster and smarter investment decisions with precision and confidence. We are a forward\-thinking company and strive to make an impact by enabling our customers to envision future possibilities, invest in ideas and infrastructure that build strong communities.
   

  

**Job Overview**
 Data is core to Preqin’s industry offerings, and the data and research management team delivers accurate, timely and complete data enhancing the value of our award\-winning offerings. The trust in our underlying content enables our clients to make key investment decisions.  Besides collecting content, the data management team collaborates extensively with our internal and external clients to improvise our offerings leveraging technology and subject matter expertise. The team understands the life cycle of data and establishes business intelligence reports seeking operational excellence.
   

  

 The individual is responsible for aiding in the future growth and development of the team’s internal and external services as well as assisting with the ongoing development of its team members.
   

  

**This role based in Japan or Hong Kong will form a core part of the Investors – Japan team.**
* This is achieved through operational excellence and clear day\-to\-day maintenance of the dataset \& content, including core business\-as\-usual (BAU) tasks and going above and beyond to collaborate, innovate and execute on relevant ad\-hoc projects to improve the accuracy, completeness and timeliness of data.


**Role Responsibilities**
* Establish and nurture relationships with key industry professionals of all levels within the alternative assets to obtain valuable proprietary information and insights regarding market sentiment and investment strategies
* Conduct face\-to\-face interactions with clients, demonstrate high standard of professionalism and subject matter expertise
* Provide tailored research and data support to clients and internal stakeholders, helping drive engagement and retention
* Respond to client inquiries with precision and professionalism, offering consultative guidance on data usage and methodologies
* Assist in onboarding new clients by providing data walkthroughs and market insights
* Maintain and enhance the accuracy, completeness, and timeliness of investor profiles within Preqin’s database. Perform rigorous data validation and cross\-checking using public filings, regulatory disclosures, and direct communications
* Troubleshooting escalated data quality incidents, collaborating with regional stakeholders and/or global BUs as needed
* Demonstrating subject matter expert (SME) for the dataset, supporting internal and external stakeholders in gaining an understanding of internal processes concerning data management
* Assisting in managing day\-to\-day data operations with an aim to create and further operational excellence
* Provide support to Research Insights, Product \& Marketing Groups, etc., to ensure all related publications and content are delivered according to guidelines and deadlines
* Supporting senior team members on research projects and getting involved in further data enhancement and development activities


**Qualifications**
* At least 2 years of relevant working experience in data/financial data services is required, preferably within alternative assets space
* Proven track record of engaging with institutional clients and high\-level executives in finance industry
* Confident presenting market intelligence, managing client relationships and handling sensitive information
* Exceptional interpersonal skills with a proven ability to build trust, credibility, and long\-term rapport with clients and industry stakeholders
* Proven ability to understand client needs and apply a consultative approach to deliver effective solutions
* Solid understanding of various investor types and key financial concepts across private markets
* Prior experience working in or knowledge of the alternative assets industry or prior expertise within Data Management in financial services is preferred
* Effective time management and prioritization skills, with a focus on continuous improvement and operational excellence
* Intellectual curiosity, strong attention to detail, and a proactive approach to identifying and solving problems
* Familiarity with CRM platforms like Salesforce and Outreach. Proficiency in data visualization tools such as Excel, SQL, Alteryx, and Power BI is a plus
* Experience working across geographies and time zones, with a collaborative and inclusive mindset
* Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced and dynamic environment
* Bachelor’s degree or higher in Finance, Business, Economics, or a STEM discipline. Advanced degrees or professional certifications (e.g., CFA, CAIA) are a plus
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both Japanese and English. Additional capability in Asian language is a plus.


**Our Benefits**
 To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.
   

  

**Our hybrid work model**
 BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.
   

  

**About BlackRock**
 At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.
   

  

 This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.

 The Asia Reporting \& Publishing Team is responsible for creating the documents which represent J.P. Morgan Asset Management’s unique brand commitments to clients around the world. The team produces client and marketing documents that articulate the performance analysis and capabilities of investment strategies and funds. This team works closely with our Product, Investment Specialist, Marketing and Client Advisor partners to create, curate and maintain client documents.
   

  

 As an Analyst in the Client Reporting Team, you will be part of the team to focus on establishing \& producing consistent client communication reports and documents, ensuring a high and consistent standard of quality deliverables to our clients, and measuring and driving client satisfaction and a consistent client experience, in close partnership with all of our internal stakeholders
   

  

 The Asia Pacific (ex\-Japan) Reporting \& Publishing Analyst role offers the opportunity to aid the region in the creation and delivery of client materials to a diverse client and stakeholder base.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Consolidate the book of work from various internal partners including: Marketing, Investment Specialists, Operations, and Client Services into a centralized Center of Excellence for Reporting and Publishing
* Participate in digital platform testing
* Drive efficiencies wherever possible
* Structure proper documentation on an ongoing basis
* Partner with key regional stakeholders, including Investment Desks, Technology, PAG, Marketing and Operations


**Required Qualifications, Skills And Capabilities**
* Excellent writing skills in English \& Chinese; fluent spoken English \& Chinese (Mandarin/Cantonese)
* Sensitivity to different cultures and working styles; ability to navigate seamlessly both across countries within APAC, and with the wider global organization
* Confidence to work directly with stakeholders at all levels
* Outstanding accuracy and attention to detail; strong numeracy
* Ability to work under pressure, and with flexibility, to meet tight deadlines
* Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel and Outlook skills (intermediate/advanced)
* University degree


**Preferred Qualifications, Skills And Capabilities**
* Prior experience of writing or participating in the Client and Fund Reporting processes is a plus
* Experience managing large data sets a plus


**ABOUT US**
 J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.
   

  

 We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.
   

  

**About The Team**
 J.P. Morgan Asset \& Wealth Management delivers industry\-leading investment management and private banking solutions. Asset Management provides individuals, advisors and institutions with strategies and expertise that span the full spectrum of asset classes through our global network of investment professionals. Wealth Management helps individuals, families and foundations take a more intentional approach to their wealth or finances to better define, focus and realize their goals.

description:
As an Arcadian, you already help us deliver world leading sustainable design, engineering, and consultancy solutions for natural and built assets. You are part of our global business comprising 36,000 people, in over 70 countries, dedicated to improving quality of life. Everyone has an important role to play. With the power of many curious minds, together we can continue to solve the world’s most complex challenges and deliver more impact together.
   

  

**Role description:**
* Our Consultant work at the very heart of every project, making sure that teams and clients work together efficiently, stay motivated and feel empowered to do their best work. You’ll be supported as you take responsibility for delivering industry\-defining work that truly transforms the world and improves quality of life.
* You will be actively encouraged to experience a variety of service lines and sectors and to develop a true understanding about what we can offer our clients. Working with teams from various disciplines you will be expected to own deliverables, liaise with clients and contribute to all aspects of project delivery, managing the success of projects through clear strategy and proactive commitment.
* Ability to take a simple brief from a Consultant/Senior Consultant /or Manager and help deliver project solutions that support the project outcome
* Provide project activity coordination, reviewing all current and planned project activity, tracking resources assigned to each project, and liaison with customers to ensure minimal impact to Client
* Assessment of project delivery success from an operational and customer perspective
* Develop capability in application of Arcadis processes and tools
* Managing Client, external stakeholders and team relationships in related activity area and/or workstream


**Qualifications \& Experience:**
* Apply with your latest transcript, DSE/IB/IELTS/TOEFL results, resume along with details of your expected salary and preferred start date. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
* Degree in Building Surveying / Building Services / Interior Design / Quantity Surveying / Engineering / Architectural / Project Management / Account Management / Construction Management / Finance or relevant discipline
* Require good competency in communication and planning experiences
* Intermediate to fluent in English, both written and spoken
* Proficient in using MS Applications
* Confident communicators and influencers
* Innovative and creative lateral thinkers
* People who are passionate about solving the industry’s main problems and ‘improving quality of life’


**Continue your career journey as an Arcadian.**
 We can only achieve our goals when everyone is empowered to be their best. We believe everyone's contribution matters. It’s why we are pioneering a skills based approach, where you can harness your unique experience and expertise to carve your career path and maximize the impact we can make together.
   

  

 You do meaningful work, and no matter where your next role in Arcadis takes you, you’ll be helping to deliver sustainable solutions for a more prosperous planet. Make your mark; on your career, your colleagues, your clients, your life and the world around you.
   

  

 Together, we can create a lasting legacy.
   

  

**Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging**
 We want you to be able to bring your best self to work every day, which is why we take equality and inclusion seriously and hold ourselves to account for our actions. Our ambition is to be an employer of choice and provide a great place to work for all our people.
   

  

 Join Arcadis. Create a legacy.

description:
We are seeking a talented individual to join our MMB team at Mercer. This role will be based in Hong Kong. This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three days a week in the office.
   

  

 The incumbent will work with a wide range of companies to help them assess, develop and deploy strategies, programs and initiatives to improve the health and wellness of their employees. He/she will advise clients on best practices, financial management, compliance, communication and administration of health and benefit plans. As an individual contributor, a Benefits Analyst is ready to apply the knowledge acquired via an advanced education and to start to develop professional skills with the help of a manager and more experienced team members.
   

  

**We will rely on you to:**
* Collect the claims data, loss ratio, member census and other renewal related documents from incumbent or prospect insurers.
* Assist the team to prepare the RFP and follow up on the quotes from the providers.
* Review the quotes received to ensure the benefits, terms and conditions matched with the RFP.
* Update the costing table, benefits schedule, terms \& conditions and other renewal information for the team to review.
* Ensure all the information received are properly file in the share folder.
* Assist the team to prepare the renewal confirmation to insurers, e.g. placing slip, accrual notice etc. for peer review.
* Send the confirmation to the providers after the completion of the peer review system (PRS).
* Support the team in other activities, e.g. prepare the reports and other ad hoc projects etc.
* Become knowledgeable about all the insurance carrier products and programs.


**What you need to have:**
* University degree in a related discipline or other relevant qualifications.
* 1\+ years of experience in administration, preferably from a client servicing industry.
* Strong track record in client and vendor management.
* Self\-initiative and curiosity in insurance/ health sector. Self\-motivated to seek out knowledge, test ideas and ways to contribute to the team.
* Ability to build relationships by communicating, influencing and negotiating effectively with team members.
* Strong language skills in English and Cantonese. Mandarin would be a plus.


**Why join our team:**
* We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders.
* We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities.
* Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well\-being.


 Mercer is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information about Mercer, visit mercer.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X.
   

  

 Marsh is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.
   

  

 Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office\-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.

 . You’ll be responsible for maintaining and expanding macroeconomic content in the Macrobond application. **What do we offer:**



* Consistent scope of responsibilities

 At Macrobond, diversity, equity, and inclusion are fundamental to our culture and success. We are committed to fostering an environment where all individuals are respected, valued, and empowered to contribute their unique perspectives.
 



 We are an equal opportunity employer.  Robert Walters is the world’s most trusted talent solutions business. Across the globe, we deliver recruitment, outsourcing, and talent advisory services for businesses of all sizes, opening doors for people with diverse skills, ambitions, and backgrounds.
 



  





 Our Outsourcing service delivers recruitment for some of the world’s top employers, and we are currently recruiting on behalf of one our client which is the is a French multinational universal bank and financial services holding company. description:
**About Jefferies** 




 Jefferies is a leading global, full\-service investment banking and capital markets firm that provides advisory, sales and trading, research and wealth and asset management services. With more than 47 offices in 21 countries around the world, we offer insights and expertise to investors, companies and governments.
 



  





 Our talented professionals provide a full range of advisory and underwriting services through creative and idea\-driven M\&A, leveraged finance, equity \& equity\-linked financing and restructuring \& recapitalization solutions. With approximately 1,300 investment banking professionals we provide global coverage across all products and sectors.
 



  





**Private Capital Advisory** 




 Jefferies Private Capital Advisory provides a full range of liquidity and fundraising solutions to financial sponsors and limited partners. We combine premier Secondary Advisory and Private Fund Advisory practices in an integrated, differentiated model \- serving as an end\-to\-end provider of all financial sponsors’ and LPs’ needs. We advise leading financial sponsors and institutional investors on secondary market transactions.
 



  





**Responsibilities may include, but are not limited to:** 



* Performing financial analysis and valuation, including leveraged buyout analysis, comparable company, and transaction‑based analyses
* Building, maintaining, and utilizing financial models to assess business performance, capital structures, and investment outcomes
* Preparing client and investor materials, including pitchbooks and information memoranda, for live transactions and marketing efforts
* Conducting industry, sector, and company research, and analyzing detailed corporate and financial information
* Supporting the execution of private capital and structured financing transactions, including coordination of diligence and interaction with internal and external stakeholders



  





**Join Our Team** 




 Off\-Cycle Interns within our Private Capital Advisory team will have the unique opportunity to gain insight into a global, client\-focused investment bank and experience an entrepreneurial environment where employees have the opportunity to make an immediate impact and gain industry exposure. Our Interns are exposed to Jefferies’ full range of top\-tier investment banking products and leading industry expertise by working closely with senior bankers on pitches and live deals. .
 



  





 In addition to performing fundamental analysis and research into companies and markets, Interns are challenged to think creatively, offer their input, and add value to the team from day one. As with everyone at Jefferies, our Interns are expected to pursue their projects with excellence, a collaborative spirit, and with the highest levels of integrity.
 



  





 We look for candidates who are eager to make an impact by doing real, hands\-on work and participating on active deal teams. Successful Jefferies Interns are able to multitask and manage a variety of projects simultaneously. Candidates must demonstrate professionalism and client management skills together with strong technical, written and verbal communication skills. The work is fast paced, challenging and intellectually stimulating. The ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines, be adaptable and work both independently and as part of a team are essential traits of a successful candidate.
 



  





 Although a background in accounting or finance can be helpful, we hire candidates who have studied a variety of disciplines. A few more application particulars are enclosed below:
 



  




* **We encourage applications from candidates who have recently graduated or have met the graduation requirements for their current degree program**
 .
* Strong academic performance in an undergraduate or postgraduate program; coursework in finance, economics, accounting, or related fields is beneficial, though candidates from diverse academic backgrounds are encouraged to apply.
* Demonstrated analytical capability, with comfort working with financial data, identifying key drivers, and synthesizing insights.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, with the ability to produce clear, accurate analysis and professional presentation materials.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills, coupled with a high level of attention to detail and organization.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced, deadline‑driven environment, with a collaborative mindset and eagerness to learn.



  





**Community \& Engagement, and Reasonable Accommodations** 




 At Jefferies, we have made a commitment to build a culture that provides opportunities for all employees regardless of our differences, and supporting a workforce that is reflective of the communities where we work and live. As a result, we are able to pool our collective insights and intelligence to provide fresh and innovative thinking for our clients.
 



  





 Jefferies is an equal employment opportunity workplace. We practice equal opportunity for all, regardless of race, religion, ancestry, color, gender, pregnancy, age, physical or mental disability or medical condition, national origin,
 
 marital, family and social status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, religion, trade union membership or any other factor protected by law. We are committed to hiring the most qualified applicants and complying with all federal, state, and local equal employment opportunity laws. In accordance with applicable law, we are dedicated to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as any mental health or physical disability needs. Please inform your recruiter should you require accommodations throughout your application process.

 **SEA Sailing Internship Programme,**

 Our Programme Is Designed With a Focus On “Result \- Oriented Learning” And “Talent Transformation”. Through The 8\- Week Internship, The Interns Will Gain Practical Work Experience Related To The SEA Business, Supplemented By Training And Mentorship. The Major Highlights Include

 Interns will engage in hands on assignment tailored to the real\-world business scenarios, gaining practical experience in SEA markets. Through diversified training, they will develop essential skills and industry knowledge to prepare for future career opportunities.

 06 July 2026 – 28 August 2026 (8 weeks)

* Personal Banking
* Corporate Banking
* Financial Markets
* Risk Management

**AI Engineer – Trading Analytics \& Data Platforms** 




**London (Hybrid – 3 days/week onsite)** 




**6 Month Contract Role (Inside IR35 – Competitive Day Rate** 




**About the Role** 




  





 We are seeking an experienced AI Engineer with a strong data engineering foundation and a passion for solving real\-world trading problems. You will work directly with traders and analysts, building AI\-powered analytics and data solutions that deliver actionable insights from market pricing and fundamental data—often in near real\-time.
 



  





 This role sits at the intersection of AI, data engineering, and trading, with a focus on rapid prototyping, stakeholder collaboration, and production\-grade delivery using modern Azure and Databricks ecosystems.
 



  





**What You’ll Do** 



* Design and deliver AI\-driven analytics for front\-office use, including forecasting, seasonality, correlation, regression, and scenario modelling
* Build scalable, reusable data pipelines using Databricks (PySpark/Spark, Delta, Unity Catalog), optimizing performance, cost, and reliability
* Develop real\-time and near real\-time data solutions to support trading and reporting needs
* Translate complex trading problems into prototypes and MVPs, iterating rapidly based on feedback
* Partner closely with traders and analysts to understand requirements and communicate insights effectively
* Implement LLM and agent\-based workflows (prompt engineering, orchestration, retrieval, tool usage, and guardrails)
* Perform statistical and econometric analysis on large\-scale time\-series datasets
* Productionize solutions with robust testing, observability, CI/CD pipelines, and documentation
* Enable reporting and data access via tools such as Power BI and similar platforms



  





**What You’ll Bring** 



* Strong hands\-on experience with Databricks and Spark (PySpark, SQL, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog)
* Proven data engineering expertise (data ingestion, modelling, orchestration, performance tuning)
* Solid foundation in statistics, econometrics, or data science—particularly with market time\-series data
* Experience building AI/ML and LLM\-based solutions (prompting, retrieval, agent workflows)
* Proficiency in Python and modern data/ML tooling (e.g., MLflow, feature stores, vector databases)
* Familiarity with CI/CD, Terraform, and production\-grade engineering practices
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work directly with front\-office users



  





**Nice to Have** 



* Experience in commodity or financial trading environments
* Understanding of market fundamentals, derivatives, P\&L, contracts, and trading lifecycle
* Knowledge of market microstructure, supply\-demand dynamics, and risk management concepts



  





**Ways of Working** 



* Hybrid model with close collaboration alongside trading teams
* Fast\-paced, iterative delivery: prototype quickly, refine with users, and scale to production
* Strong focus on engineering excellence, including automated testing, governance, and operational reliability
* Ability to work independently while leveraging support from a broader data and engineering team



  





**Who You Are** 



* A pragmatic problem\-solver who thrives in ambiguous, high\-impact environments
* Comfortable bridging the gap between technical solutions and trading needs
* Driven to deliver measurable value through AI and data



  





**If you’re excited about applying AI and advanced analytics in a trading environment and working directly with front\-office stakeholders, we’d love to hear from you.**

**The Opportunity**
 The Portfolio Management Group sits at the heart of Hamilton Lane’s portfolio journey with investors.

+ Landscape of European private credit, comparisons to US private credit and how investors can build better portfolios combining European private credit alongside US private credit

title: Graduate program, Finance
matched_skills: Supply Chain Management, Inventory Management & Optimization, Data Governance, Python, SQL, PySpark, SAP (ERP), Financial Planning and Control, Due diligence
gaps_in_skill: US GAAP and IFRS Alignment, M&A Integration, Investor Relations and Fundraising Support
description:
At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.
   

  

 In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.
   

  

 For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.
   

  

 We close on acquisitions, restructure entities, and consolidate financials across jurisdictions—all at a pace that would break most finance teams. We need someone who finds that exciting, not exhausting.
   

  

 This is a graduate program that will flexibly expose you to a variety of projects, spanning from the internal financial management of the company to the integration of newly acquired businesses into our financial infrastructure. You'll work on genuinely hard problems—multi\-entity consolidations, acquisition due diligence, US GAAP and IFRS alignment—and build the technical foundations to grow rapidly into a finance professional. If you’re the kind of person who wants to learn fast, take on difficult finance problems early, and build the judgment and technical depth required to grow into an exceptional finance professional, you’ll thrive here.
   

  

**A few examples of your responsibilities** 
* **Technical accounting at real depth.** 
 Prepare and own financial statements under US GAAP, with IFRS alignment where required. Exercise judgment on revenue recognition, consolidation, and intercompany flows—in a context where the reporting framework evolves constantly as we acquire and integrate new businesses.
* **Financial planning and control.** 
 Produce reporting that actually drives decisions, collaborate with cross\-functional teams on budget management, and ensure fiscal policies hold up as the business scales.
* **Due diligence and post\-M\&A integration.** 
 Support financial and tax due diligence for acquisitions and related restructuring activities. Contribute to post\-M\&A integration, ensuring smooth financial, operational, and reporting alignment.
* **Investor relations and fundraising support.** 
 Help prepare board meetings and minutes. Own financing to support M\&A, engaging with banks, investors, and institutional stakeholders.
* **Equity plans.** 
 Manage and optimize equity plans—implement, support team members on an ongoing basis, and evolve the program as it grows.


**What we look for** 
* **Reasoning ability.** 
 Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.
* **Drive.** 
 You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.
* **Team spirit.** 
 You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.
* **Proficiency in English.** 
 You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.


**What we offer** 
* **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** 
 You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.
* **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** 
 We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.
* **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** 
 Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.
* **A flexible start date and part\-time options.** 
 You don't need to wait until graduation to apply. We offer flexible start dates and the possibility to begin part\-time, transitioning to full\-time as you complete your degree. Many Spooners joined before graduating and progressively took on greater responsibility, with arrangements that allowed them to do so without compromising their education.
* **All. These. Benefits.** 
 Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.


**Commitment \& contract**
 Fixed\-term or internship. Full\-time or part\-time.
   

  

**Location**
 Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.
   

  

**The selection process**
 In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.
   

  

 If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.
   

  

 We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.
   

  

 We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.
   

  

**Before you apply**
 If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.
   

  

 Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.
   

  

 To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.
   

  

 If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

description:
**Job Description**
 Are you ready to make an impact in a fast\-paced trading environment? At JPMorgan Chase, you’ll play a key role in managing complex derivative financing risks and supporting business decisions. Our Differential Discounting Product Control team is at the forefront of risk management, financial reporting, and regulatory compliance. You’ll collaborate with senior leaders and drive meaningful change across the organization. This is your opportunity to grow your expertise and advance your career in a dynamic, global financial institution.
   

  

 As a Differential Discounting Product Control Associate in the IB Finance and Business Management team, you help us manage CSA\-specific discounting risks and ensure accurate financial and regulatory reporting. You work closely with trading desks, business management, and support functions to deliver key business insights. You drive the control agenda, support technology and regulatory initiatives, and foster strong relationships across the organization. Together, we create a robust control environment and support strategic business decisions.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Reconcile and report profit and loss figures from dealer estimates to General Ledger postings
* Analyze and report key business risk measures for the Differential Discounting business
* Own and validate inputs to Value\-at\-Risk (VaR) calculations
* Ensure end\-to\-end integrity of deal data on bank systems
* Investigate and resolve discrepancies between front and back\-office systems
* Advise the business on accounting, regulatory, and booking issues
* Participate in new product approval and sign off business unit financial statements
* Support business unit strategy and management with financial analysis
* Develop and maintain relationships with senior Front Office, Business Management, and support functions
* Liaise with business and risk managers to further the control agenda
* Adapt to changing priorities in a fast\-paced, trading and month\-end environment


**Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills**
* Strong numerical and analytical skills
* Understanding of interest rate products, including FRA, IR and XCCY Swaps, tenor/currency basis, and discounting calculations
* Proficiency in spreadsheets (MS Excel), including formulae and macro construction or willingness to learn
* Ability to interact effectively in a trading room environment and develop partnerships across teams
* Proficiency in accounting and knowledge of US and UK GAAP
* Solid understanding of internal controls and risk assessment concepts
* Self\-starter with a strong interest in learning
* Ability to work well under pressure, with attention to detail and tenacity
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Strong multi\-tasking and organizational skills
* Relevant experience in finance, product control, or risk management


**Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills**
* Experience with regulatory, credit, market risk, tax, and accounting issues related to derivatives
* Familiarity with technology systems supporting trading and risk management
* Experience supporting technology, business, or regulatory initiatives
* Ability to balance cooperative and responsive approaches with rigorous control standards
* Experience in a fast\-paced trading or month\-end environment
* Demonstrated subject matter expertise in product control
* Advanced Excel skills, including VBA/macros


**ABOUT US**
 J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.
   

  

 We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs.  J.P. Morgan’s Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

description:
**Finance Systems Analyst \| £55,000 \- £65,000**
 We're working with a leading, fast\-growing Hospitality Group on this exciting opportunity. This is a high\-impact role for a tech\-savvy accountant to take full ownership of the financial systems landscape while driving commercial FP\&A and automation initiatives.
   

  

 You will be the bridge between finance and technology, leveraging tools like Excel (VBA/Macros), ERP systems, and BI reporting modules to streamline operations for a dynamic group. This role is perfect for a proactive analyst who thrives on troubleshooting complex data sets and implementing scalable system enhancements to support a rapidly expanding portfolio.
   

  

**The Role**
* Full ownership of financial systems administration, safeguarding data integrity, and conducting regular system upgrades and maintenance.
* Lead automation projects to eliminate manual bottlenecks in month\-end and year\-end reporting processes.
* Drive Group FP\&A activities, including budgeting, forecasting, and the development of sophisticated financial models.
* Act as the key contact for finance master data governance, ensuring consistency across multiple legal entities and business units.
* Partner with senior stakeholders to deliver actionable performance insights and troubleshoot system\-related queries.


**What You'll Need**
* Professional Accounting Qualification (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) or qualified by experience (QBE) with a heavy systems focus.
* Proven experience in finance systems management, data migration, or ERP implementation (e.g., NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics, SAP, or Sage Intacct).
* Advanced technical proficiency in Excel, including data manipulation, complex formulas, and financial modelling.
* Exposure to the Hospitality, Retail, or Leisure sector is highly desirable, or experience managing systems in a fast\-paced multi\-site environment.
* A proactive, analytical mindset with the ability to communicate technical system issues to non\-technical finance stakeholders.


**What's On Offer**
* Competitive salary of £55,000 \- £65,000 based on experience.
* Hybrid, remote\-friendly culture offering great work\-life balance in the heart of London.
* Direct mentorship from highly regarded finance leaders committed to your long\-term career progression.
* The chance to work in a dynamic, social industry with a company that is aggressively expanding its market footprint.


**Apply via Haystack today!**

description:
**About This Role**
 BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms and a premier provider of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional, intermediary and individual investors around the world. BlackRock offers a range of solutions — from rigorous fundamental and quantitative active management approaches aimed at maximizing outperformance to highly efficient indexing strategies designed to gain broad exposure to the world’s capital markets. Our clients can access our investment solutions through a variety of product structures, including individual and institutional separate accounts, mutual funds and other pooled investment vehicles, and the industry\-leading iShares® ETFs.
   

  

**Business Unit Overview**
 The International Cash Management Group manages a suite of products that includes Prime and Government Money Market and Ultra\-Short Duration Mutual funds and Separate Accounts. BlackRock’s cash management style is designed to meet each client’s objectives of capital preservation, liquidity and yield – in that order. This philosophy is built on a risk\-controlled framework, featuring careful analysis of credit, interest rate trends, and relative value opportunities which ultimately produce investment returns on a risk\-adjusted basis. Our disciplined approach to investing originated in 1973 when we pioneered the first constant\-dollar money market fund dedicated to institutional investors.
   

  

**Job Purpose/Background/Responsibilities**
 This role sits within the International Cash Portfolio Management Team and is primarily a junior portfolio management / trader role based in Singapore or Hong Kong. The successful candidate will spend a large part of the day as a dealer supporting the international cash investment process by investing in money market instruments (Time Deposits, Reverse Repo, Commercial Paper, Certificates of Deposit, short Government bonds and Bills, Supranationals and any other suitable instruments), with an emphasis on major Asian currencies. The person will also play a major role in collating data and using technology to help improve desk processes and analysis of portfolios and positions. A natural curiosity and drive will help the successful candidate establish themselves within a busy and proactive team, helping research markets, regulation, credit, technology and ESG.
   

  

* To execute daily trading of money market instruments in support of the Lead Portfolio Managers with a focus on Time Deposits and government bills
* To understand and contribute to the strategy and construction of HKD / USD and other Asian currency money market Funds as well as separately managed accounts
* To produce analytics and portfolio level data for strategy, client and other key meetings
* To build and maintain strong professional relationships with external trading counterparties (brokers and dealer banks) and other parts of the broader ecosystem (rating agencies, etc.)
* To build strong partnerships with internal stakeholders such as credit, product strategy, risk, operations, pricing, legal \& compliance, and contribute to our diverse and inclusive work environment
* To work with other data and technology focused colleagues to help create greater operational efficiencies across the desk


**Knowledge / Experience Required**
* Ideally 3\-5 years of work experience in cash/short\-duration fixed income portfolio management or trading, and transferable skills to benefit this role.
* An enthusiastic attitude with a willingness to learn
* Ability to multi\-task and prioritize tasks in a demanding and fast\-paced environment
* Foundational knowledge of financial markets and understanding of credit and risk management
* An interest in developing research, quantitative or analytical skills, particularly using data systems (Knowledge of Aladdin a plus)
* Knowledge and understanding of digital assets and tokenised real\-world assets preferred
* Experience with Python, SQL, Tableau, Bloomberg, VBA a plus
* An interest in developing presentation and written communication skills
* Interpersonal skills to help foster an inclusive culture of teamwork and knowledge sharing
* Fluent in English. Cantonese or Mandarin preferred but not mandatory.


**Our Benefits**
 To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.
   

  

**Our hybrid work model**
 BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.
   

  

**About BlackRock**
 At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.
   

  

 This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.
   

  

 For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock
   

  

 BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, family status, gender identity, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected attributes at law.

 Commanding relevant industry experience, strong communications, coordination and problem\-solving skills, the RA will work closely with the business team on a portfolio of institutional relationships. **Introduction** 


  
 




 Temasek's Cybersecurity department is looking to hire full\-time interns to support various cybersecurity initiatives across different teams. This internship will give you an opportunity to gain first\-hand experience in planning and executing cybersecurity projects while developing valuable technical and business skills in a global investment company.
 

  
 





  
 

  
 





 The department comprises of 4 key technical pillars: (1\) Defence and Resilience, (2\) Governance and Oversight, (3\) Architecture and Engineering Operations, and (4\) Innovation, Incubation \& Initiatives. Specific domain requirements are listed below, but interns would have the opportunity for cross\-functional learning and experience.
 

  
 




**1\.Defence and Resilience Intern** 


  
 



  
 


* This role will assist in supporting the uplift of Temasek’s cybersecurity resilience through development and execution of an effective cyber resilience programme and establishment of new/advanced cyber capabilities to strengthen cyber awareness, enhance cyber readiness and monitoring capabilities. This enables us to driving a culture of cyber resilience across Temasek and its ecosystem.
* Drive security awareness through innovative and engaging methods, creating a culture where cybersecurity becomes second nature
* Develop compelling technical content for
 

 both technical and non\-technical audiences
* Design interactive training programs that transform complex security concepts into actionable behaviours
* Plan and
 

 facilitate realistic tabletop exercises that prepare operational and management teams for real\-world 
 

 cyber
 

  threats
* Monitor effectiveness of existing awareness programmes gather feedback, and recommend enhancements for continuous improvement



**2\. Governance and Oversight Intern** 


  
 


* This role will assist in supporting continuous enhancement and implementation of an effective governance and risk management framework to manage technology, data security, and cyber risks across the enterprise. This will enable Temasek to be better prepared to mitigate and manage these risks in the face of evolving cybersecurity threats.
* Perform Governance, Risk and Compliance related duties including 
 

 policy reviews, risk assessments, and control testing
* Identify gaps in governance frameworks and propose enhancements to strengthen oversight
* Provide support to GRC related reviews and processes
* Track audit findings and 
 

 follow up with IT partners/vendors on audit/assurance findings
* Maintain accurate records of compliance processes risk registers, and assurance reports



**3\. Architecture and Engineering Operations Intern (Security Operations)** 


  
 


* This role will assist in supporting Temasek’s security operations through monitoring and compliance checks, streamlining documentation, coordinating incident response, enhancing governance to address operational risks, and providing project management support for ongoing initiatives.
* Perform ongoing monitoring on security operations hygiene, controls compliance by leveraging variety of management tools and techniques to detect operational irregularities and lapses.
* Support development, maintenance and streamline documentation of operational SOPs, playbooks, configuration baselines, operational reports to improve operational efficiency and effectiveness.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure prompt and efficient incident response and support containment activities.
* Drive and improve security governance to identify operational gaps and risk across the security solution stack.
* Provide project management support in ongoing initiatives


**4\. Architecture and Engineering Operations Intern (Architecture)** 

  
 


* This role will assist in supporting in the development and execution of Temasek’s cybersecurity defense strategy and planning for future cybersecurity needs.
* Support security technology evaluations and proof\-of\-concept
 

 for security solutions, documenting findings and recommendations
* Research emerging security threats, technologies, 
 

 vulnerabilities, and industry trends
* Assist in creating technical security documentation, diagrams and presentations 
 

 to support solution design and stakeholder communication



**5\. Innovation, Incubation \& Initiatives Intern** 


  
 


* Assist the team by researching and evaluating emerging technology risks (e.g., quantum computing, agentic AI) and identifying potential security use\-cases and mitigations.
* Support initiatives to enhance detection of hard\-to\-detect threats by exploring advanced solutions (e.g., automation, advanced analytics) to improve alert quality, triage speed, and mitigation workflows.
* Additional scope may include supporting the establishment of an Insider Risk \& Security (IRS) capability to improve visibility of insider threats and enable timely response actions.



**Requirements**

* Please indicate your availability (start and end date) and expected graduation date.
* University undergraduates pursuing any of these majors/degrees: Computer Science, Information Security, Information Systems, Cybersecurity, IT Management.
* We also welcome applicants from other disciplines with demonstrated interest in cybersecurity.



  
 




**What we are looking for**





  
 


* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Prior internship experience(s) with demonstrated capabilities in cybersecurity, IT security or related fields will be considered a strong plus.
* Adaptive in fast paced environment and able to multi\-task and handle complex projects with multiple stakeholders.

title: Portfolio Management - Cash Management Group - Associate
matched_skills: Python, SQL, Tableau, Data Governance, Financial Modeling
gaps_in_skill: Bloomberg, VBA, Aladdin knowledge, Specific fixed income portfolio management experience
description:
**About This Role**
 BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms and a premier provider of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional, intermediary and individual investors around the world. BlackRock offers a range of solutions — from rigorous fundamental and quantitative active management approaches aimed at maximizing outperformance to highly efficient indexing strategies designed to gain broad exposure to the world’s capital markets. Our clients can access our investment solutions through a variety of product structures, including individual and institutional separate accounts, mutual funds and other pooled investment vehicles, and the industry\-leading iShares® ETFs.
   

  

**Business Unit Overview**
 The International Cash Management Group manages a suite of products that includes Prime and Government Money Market and Ultra\-Short Duration Mutual funds and Separate Accounts. BlackRock’s cash management style is designed to meet each client’s objectives of capital preservation, liquidity and yield – in that order. This philosophy is built on a risk\-controlled framework, featuring careful analysis of credit, interest rate trends, and relative value opportunities which ultimately produce investment returns on a risk\-adjusted basis. Our disciplined approach to investing originated in 1973 when we pioneered the first constant\-dollar money market fund dedicated to institutional investors.
   

  

**Job Purpose/Background/Responsibilities**
 This role sits within the International Cash Portfolio Management Team and is primarily a junior portfolio management / trader role based in Singapore or Hong Kong. The successful candidate will spend a large part of the day as a dealer supporting the international cash investment process by investing in money market instruments (Time Deposits, Reverse Repo, Commercial Paper, Certificates of Deposit, short Government bonds and Bills, Supranationals and any other suitable instruments), with an emphasis on major Asian currencies. The person will also play a major role in collating data and using technology to help improve desk processes and analysis of portfolios and positions. A natural curiosity and drive will help the successful candidate establish themselves within a busy and proactive team, helping research markets, regulation, credit, technology and ESG.
   

  

* To execute daily trading of money market instruments in support of the Lead Portfolio Managers with a focus on Time Deposits and government bills
* To understand and contribute to the strategy and construction of HKD / USD and other Asian currency money market Funds as well as separately managed accounts
* To produce analytics and portfolio level data for strategy, client and other key meetings
* To build and maintain strong professional relationships with external trading counterparties (brokers and dealer banks) and other parts of the broader ecosystem (rating agencies, etc.)
* To build strong partnerships with internal stakeholders such as credit, product strategy, risk, operations, pricing, legal \& compliance, and contribute to our diverse and inclusive work environment
* To work with other data and technology focused colleagues to help create greater operational efficiencies across the desk


**Knowledge / Experience Required**
* Ideally 3\-5 years of work experience in cash/short\-duration fixed income portfolio management or trading, and transferable skills to benefit this role.
* An enthusiastic attitude with a willingness to learn
* Ability to multi\-task and prioritize tasks in a demanding and fast\-paced environment
* Foundational knowledge of financial markets and understanding of credit and risk management
* An interest in developing research, quantitative or analytical skills, particularly using data systems (Knowledge of Aladdin a plus)
* Knowledge and understanding of digital assets and tokenised real\-world assets preferred
* Experience with Python, SQL, Tableau, Bloomberg, VBA a plus
* An interest in developing presentation and written communication skills
* Interpersonal skills to help foster an inclusive culture of teamwork and knowledge sharing
* Fluent in English. Cantonese or Mandarin preferred but not mandatory.


**Our Benefits**
 To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.
   

  

**Our hybrid work model**
 BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.
   

  

**About BlackRock**
 At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.
   

  

 This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.
   

  

 For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock
   

  

 BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, family status, gender identity, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected attributes at law.

description:
**Job Description**
 Step into the heart of Asia’s dynamic markets as an Analyst on our Asia Macro Strategy team. In this junior research role, you’ll be instrumental in driving our research agenda and delivering impactful insights to clients. If you’re passionate about macroeconomics, financial markets, and the fast\-evolving APAC region, this is an exciting opportunity to launch your career and develop specialized expertise. With APAC as a major growth engine for our business, we’re looking for a high\-caliber candidate eager to make a difference.
   

  

 As a Macro Strategy Analyst in Wealth Management, you’ll collaborate with a global network of Strategists, Economists, and Asset Class Specialists. Your responsibilities will include performing in\-depth analysis, conducting research, and crafting reports that articulate J.P. Morgan Private Bank’s investment perspectives. You’ll support senior Strategists in shaping core macroeconomic views, generating investment ideas, and developing asset allocation strategies. Your day\-to\-day will involve data analysis, content publication, and trade idea generation, with a clear path to building specialized expertise over time.

* Conduct research and analysis on economic and financial market developments, supporting the generation of actionable investment recommendations.
* Assist in formulating macro and asset class views, with a focus on Asia macroeconomics as well as global interest rates and currencies.
* Contribute to the creation of publications and market commentaries for clients, the media, and internal stakeholders, helping to communicate J.P. Morgan Private Bank’s perspectives and support informed investment decisions.
* Present economic and market analysis, along with investment recommendations, to both internal teams and external clients.
* Collaborate with senior team members on ad\-hoc research projects and contribute to the development of proprietary models and analytical tools.
* Stay up to date on key macroeconomic data releases, policy developments, and market\-moving events.
